name: Bhai Utam Singh Hakim (Amritsar)
body: Bhai Utam Singh Hakim was one of the earliest recorded artists by the Gramophone Company. In the early days of the recording industry, the Gramophone Company had allowed individuals to set up music agencies in some of the larger cities. In this manner, one Lal Singh had also set up an agency in Hall Bazaar, Amritsar. He made a good amount of money and once he had around 5000 Rs. saved up, instead of settling the account with the Gramophone Company, he ran off to Japan. How this relates to Bhai Utam Singh Ji is that he also worked in Lal Singh’s music agency and he was afraid that the Gramophone Company would come after him for the unsettled tabs. As he was not very well to do, Bhai Utam Singh Ji approached Bhai Vir Singh Ji who then wrote a lengthy letter to the Gramophone Company requesting them to transfer ownership of the agency to Bhai Utam Singh Ji who was a very hardworking individual. With that, Bhai Utam Singh Ji also became the owner of this agency and he released many records. Between 1906 and 1907, he must have released at least 20 records if not more. Bhai Utam Singh Hakim also recorded the very first Ardas in 1904 and in 1910 he released an Asa Ki Vaar set as well.  Bhai Utam Singh Ji passed away some time before 1929. The above information was taken from the very informative book by Sardar Balbir Singh Kanwal, ‘Punjab De Parsidh Raagi Rababi’. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Utam Singh Hakim, please contact us.
